note: AUD5 fix for finding F-14 (docs/flowmap/audit/04-findings.md). SCOPE: test-authoring — a controlled fixture replaces data-dependence on the live plan.

intent.problem: AUD3: orchestrate.test.mjs's only blocking check (exit 1 iff FAIL) was data-dependent on the live public/plan.json state — if that plan ever became fully built, the blocking path would silently go unexercised.
intent.approach: a fixture plan adding a node whose symbol can never exist in code: orchestrate must dispatch it in wave 0, verdict it FAIL, count summary.fail=1 and exit 1 — unconditionally, independent of live repo state.
intent.rationale: a gate test must control its own inputs; the live-plan assertions stay (they cover the real artifact), the fixture guarantees the deny path forever.
